First American Specialty Insurance Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Thomas W. Purtell (Email: tpurtell@thomaswjpurtell-law.com) of the law offices of Thomas W. J. Purtell (Tel: 415.722.6291) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, David Arthur Evans (Email: devans@selvinwraith.com) of the law offices of Selvin Wraith Halman LLP (Tel: 510.874.1811) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Amended Complaint is DENIED. First, the amended complaint's third cause of action adequately pleads elder abuse; it alleges that defendant "retained monetary sums" owed to plaintiff after he turned 65. (Id. at 16:14-17:20; Welf. & Inst. Code 15610.30(a)(1).) Second, plaintiff's punitive damages allegations are sufficient for the pleading stage under Civil Code 3294 and Welfare & Institutions Code 15657.5. For example, the amended complaint alleges that defendant (a) refused to defend plaintiff in the Perez case because no wrongful eviction was alleged, when it clearly was and (b) stated that it would make payment for plaintiff's past attorney fees, but then did not. (Id. at 2:7-23, 3:10-14.) Third, the amended complaint pleads issues that could be controlled by insurer-appointed counsel such as whether plaintiff caused damage in the Perez case and thus supports independent counsel fees. (Civ. Code 2860.)

Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances by ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Thomas W. Purtell (Email: tpurtell@thomaswjpurtell-law.com) of the law offices of Thomas W. J. Purtell (Tel: 415.722.6291) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, David Arthur Evans (Email: devans@selvinwraith.com) of the law offices of Selvin Wraith Halman LLP (Tel: 510.874.1811) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FIRST AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRERS to First Amended COMPLAINT are overruled. The amended complaint adequately alleges breaches of contract and the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing under California's liberal pleading standards. (Perkins v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 1, 6.) The parties' papers raise issues of fact and mixed law and fact appropriate for a summary adjudication motion. For example, the Perez lawsuit against plaintiff pled wrongful eviction and personal injuries, while defendant said it did not. Further, plaintiff's interpretations of the insurance contract at a minimum raise ambiguities, and thus must be accepted as correct at the pleading stage. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Insurance Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Plaintiff's third cause of action adequately pleads elder abuse; it alleges that defendant "retained monetary sums" owed plaintiff after he turned 65. (Id. at 16:14-17:20; Welf. & Inst. Code 15610.30(a)(1).)

Written order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.
